name: RULE REVEAL
conditioned_path: niche deep-dive rabbit-holes where the algorithm rewards hyper-specific rule-hunting without ever surfacing the actual rule
mechanic: A sequence of colored shapes appears; tap the next shape in the pattern before the blank slot fades. The rule is never stated — you discover it by watching the sequence repeat and vary. When you've correctly predicted 10 in a row the rule surfaces as text and the session closes.
video_trick: curiosity gap — the rule is right there if you look close enough

name: SEQUENCE LOCK
conditioned_path: hyper-specific procedure loops — the forum thread that lays out the exact 12-step ritual for something obscure
mechanic: A combination lock shows 6 dials; each dial has a hidden rule for which symbol it cycles to next. Tap each dial in the correct sequence to open the lock. No guidance — pattern-watch two full cycles, then execute. Click, click, click, open. A finite vault with a clean end.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the lock clicks open

name: SORT BY RULE
conditioned_path: taxonomy rabbit-holes — the Reddit thread classifying every sub-type of a thing with rigorous criteria
mechanic: Items drift in and you sort them into bins by a rule you must infer from the first few items. Each correct sort reveals more of the rule; each wrong sort gently returns the item. When all 20 items are correctly sorted the rule prints and the tray seals.
video_trick: shock-honesty — the rule was hiding in plain sight the whole time

name: PATTERN FILL
conditioned_path: satisfying-completion loops — mosaic, pixel-art fill, or number-colour-by-number that the algorithm feeds endlessly
mechanic: A grid of cells has a hidden repeating tile pattern with gaps. Tap each empty cell to fill it in the color the pattern demands. Consistent audio click per correct fill; a wrong tap greys out. When the grid is complete it pulses once and the session ends.
video_trick: calm completion — the pattern resolves to whole

name: TRAIN TIMETABLE
conditioned_path: system-mastery loops — the algorithm that surfaces transit-nerd, schedule-nerd, or logistics-nerd content to people who love knowing exactly how a system ticks
mechanic: A timetable grid shows departure slots; drag each train token into its correct departure slot so no two trains share a platform at the same time. The rule is the constraint system itself — figure it out, fill it in, and the last train departs on a clean chime.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — every slot filled, system running smooth

name: COLOR TAXONOMY
conditioned_path: colour-theory and palette rabbit-holes — the deep-dives on hue families and complementary systems
mechanic: Swatches arrive one at a time; drag each into the correct position on a colour wheel taxonomy. There are no labels — only the relationship between neighbours tells you where a swatch belongs. A perfectly placed swatch hums softly; a wrong placement nudges back. 24 swatches, one clean wheel.
video_trick: near-perfection itch — almost right, nudge it one slot

name: PRIME SIEVE
conditioned_path: number-pattern rabbit-holes — the maths-nerd thread walking through elegant integer sequences
mechanic: A grid of numbers 1-50 lights up one by one; tap each composite to grey it out, leaving only primes glowing. There is no timer — just you and the sieve of Eratosthenes, a 2,000-year-old rule you discover (or remember) at your own pace. When all composites are greyed the primes pulse in unison.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the primes emerge from the noise

name: ECHO SEQUENCE
conditioned_path: audio-pattern loops — ASMR tap sequences and rhythmic repeat content that rewards sustained sensory attention
mechanic: A sequence of tones plays; tap the pads in the same order to echo it back. Each correct echo plays the sequence one note longer. The audio is the entire feedback loop — precise, predictable, and never random. Reach the full 12-note sequence and it resolves to a chord.
video_trick: participation-comment-bait — how far did you echo?

name: CATEGORY CENSUS
conditioned_path: classification rabbit-holes — the thread sorting every entry of a thing into a precise taxonomy with argued edge cases
mechanic: Items drop from above; tap the correct category bin for each. The bins are labelled but the items are ambiguous — a few rounds reveal which edge cases belong where. Each correctly categorised item clicks into its bin; a wrong bin gently bounces it back. 30 items, then the census is complete.
video_trick: shock-honesty — that edge case belonged in the other bin all along

name: ROUTINE BUILDER
conditioned_path: optimised-routine content — the morning-routine, workflow-setup, and system-design deep-dives the algorithm feeds to routine-mastery brains
mechanic: Drag step cards into a vertical sequence to build a routine. Each card snaps with a satisfying click; misplaced cards push back softly. A dependency checker lights green when all steps are in the correct logical order. Lock the routine and a clean summary card slides down — your system, complete.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the routine locked and running

name: GRID LOGIC
conditioned_path: logic-puzzle rabbit-holes — Sudoku, nonogram, and Picross deep-dives the algorithm feeds to constraint-solving brains
mechanic: A 5x5 grid has clues on each row and column (counts of filled cells). Tap cells to fill or unfill them until all clue constraints are satisfied. No guessing — pure deductive logic. Each satisfied row or column softly highlights. Last cell in, the grid blooms.
video_trick: near-perfection itch — one cell left, the answer is right there

name: SIGNAL SORT
conditioned_path: signal-vs-noise rabbit-holes — the thread identifying the one real pattern buried in a noisy dataset
mechanic: A stream of icons flows across the canvas; tap only the ones that match a hidden signal pattern. The noise icons are similar but subtly wrong — wrong rotation, wrong count of dots, wrong gap. Each correct tap adds a soft chime; each miss adds a subtle dissonant tone. 20 correct picks and the signal prints.
video_trick: play-along comment bait — can you spot the signal by round 5?

name: TYPE SORT
conditioned_path: typology rabbit-holes — MBTI, Enneagram, plant genus, bird species ID threads the algorithm surfaces to systemising brains
mechanic: Specimens slide in one at a time (abstract shapes with coded traits). Drag each into the correct type-bucket based on a key you build up as you go — early specimens are textbook examples, later ones are edge cases. 24 specimens. When the last one slots in, every bucket shows its complete set.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the full taxonomy displayed

name: BEAT MAP
conditioned_path: music-structure rabbit-holes — the deep-dives into song construction, verse-chorus maps, and time-signature analysis
mechanic: A song structure map loads as empty bars. Listen to a short audio loop and tap each bar to label it (intro/verse/chorus/bridge/outro) based on what you hear. Correct labels snap with a satisfying tick; mismatches push back. Map the full structure and the song plays through once more, labelled.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the map that explains the song

name: PIPELINE TRACE
conditioned_path: systems-diagram rabbit-holes — the flowchart and process-map content the algorithm feeds to pipeline-obsessed brains
mechanic: A tangle of pipe segments sits on the canvas; tap each segment to rotate it until water can flow from source to drain without leaks. Each connected segment glows; each completed path fills with a gentle flow animation. All paths flowing is the end state.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the system flows

name: ODD ONE DEEP
conditioned_path: pattern-deviation rabbit-holes — the thread cataloguing every exception to a rule in a system
mechanic: A matrix of items has one that violates the system rule. The rule is not stated. Watch the matrix for one full cycle, then tap the outlier. Each round adds a dimension (shape AND colour AND count AND rotation) — the rule grows more specific, the outlier more subtle. 10 rounds and the outlier chain resolves.
video_trick: play-along comment bait — did you catch the outlier before round 7?

name: FREQUENCY STACK
conditioned_path: data-visualisation rabbit-holes — the thread mapping frequency distributions and histograms of niche datasets
mechanic: Tokens fall and you drag each to the correct frequency column on a histogram you build in real time. The distribution has a hidden shape (bell curve, skew, bimodal) you discover only as you place items. When the last token is placed the histogram shows its shape and a label prints — you figured out the distribution.
video_trick: shock-honesty — the shape was bimodal the whole time

name: SENSE CALIBRATE
conditioned_path: sensory-processing content — threads on sound sensitivity, texture grading, and sensory preference mapping
mechanic: A soft tone plays; slide a dial to the exact frequency that matches it. The dial gives consistent haptic-style visual feedback as you approach the target — a waveform that settles to flat when aligned. 8 tones, each a different frequency. Last tone calibrated, the dial sequence plays back as a chord.
video_trick: near-perfection itch — almost flat, one hair to the left

name: MAP LEGEND
conditioned_path: cartography and classification rabbit-holes — the deep-dive on map symbology, legend systems, and geographic taxonomy
mechanic: A map displays regions marked with unknown symbols; a legend panel shows symbol definitions in scrambled order. Drag each legend label to its correct symbol. The map's symbols are consistent — once you decode one region, the pattern cascades. All labels matched, the map unlocks its full annotated view.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the map finally makes sense

name: SYSTEM CLOSE
conditioned_path: system-mastery niche loops — the deep-dive that pulls you in because you need to see the complete system before you can stop
mechanic: A 5-stage machine shows its gears; each stage has a hidden trigger condition. Discover and satisfy each condition in order — the machine advances one stage at a time with a satisfying mechanical click. All 5 stages triggered, the machine runs a full cycle and prints SYSTEM COMPLETE. Share your run count.
video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the machine runs its first clean cycle
